characters.name: Alexa
characters.description: Alexa is a good friend of Karen Brockman in series 1, mainly because Sue, Karen's mother, is always dropping Alexa off at places. Alexa and Karen get along well, and enjoy annoying Sue. Alexa is also the Daughter ofJanewho is always late to pick up Alexa. Which means Sue and Pete always has to look after Alexa, Karen, Jake and Ben.

characters.name: Angela Morrison
characters.description: Angela MorrisonLocationUnited States /'The World'AgeUnknownGenderMaleSeries InformationFirst SeenThe Special BowlLast SeenSpartacus the Musical Angela Bollinger(néeMorrison) is a fictional character in the television series,Outnumbered, and she is portrayed by Samantha Bond. Angela is Sue's older sister. She is a very 'new age' person who is into spiritual guides, dream catchers and feng shui. Having spent a long time in America after their mother's death, Angela turns up un-expectedly at Sue and Pete's house one day and, much to their surprise, announces that she is staying put for a while to look after their dad,Frankwho is ill. Angela is Sue's older sister. She is a very 'new age' person who is into spiritual guides, dream catchers and feng shui. Having spent a long time in America after their mother's death, Angela turns up un-expectedly at Sue and Pete's house one day and, much to their surprise, announces that she is staying put for a while to look after their dad,Frankwho is ill. However Angela loses Frank at the farm and is dumped by her boyfriend Trent, leaving Sue to predict that it won't be long before she is 'running back to America'. Sue turns out to be right as in the last episode of series one, she announces that she's leaving again. Angela wants to be liked by her niece and nephews and so often undermines Sue and Pete by buying them nice presents and giving them money and chocolate. Despite her bribery, it is only Ben who seems to like her. Occasionally, Angela can act in a way that can be considered snide.

characters.name: Barbara
characters.description: Barbarais a fictional character in the television series,Outnumbered, She is the Brockmans' next door neighbour, and is portrayed by Lorraine Pilkington.  Barbara is the Brockman's next door neighbour. She seems to lead a perfect life with children who go to private school and who play exceptionally unusual instruments. The family are always having fund-raising events. This starts Sue wondering why her children cannot live at the same standard as Barbara's kids and becomes gradually more jealous of them. Barbara is the Brockman's next door neighbour. She seems to lead a perfect life with children who go to private school and who play exceptionally unusual instruments. The family are always having fund-raising events. This starts Sue wondering why her children cannot live at the same standard as Barbara's kids and becomes gradually more jealous of them. However, we discover later on that life is not quite so perfect for Barbara after all - she has a number of loud rows with her husband which keeps the whole neighbourhood awake (she sent the children away to stay with her mother). The next day the police arrive and tell the Brockman family the unusual news; it turns out that she hit him over the head with a frying pan (even though he stated that it sounded all "Tom and Jerry").

characters.name: Ben Brockman
characters.description: Ben BrockmanBen in series five (the final series)Location19 Keely Road, LondonAgeUnknownGenderMaleSeries InformationFirst SeenThe School RunLast SeenChristmas 2024 Benjamin David Brockman, commonly known as Ben, is a fictional character in the British comedy seriesOutnumbered. He was born between January and August 2000. Ben is the middle child in the Brockman family, and he is a hyperactive and crafty kid. In Series 1, he had developed an extraordinary talent for lying, which his parents were clearly annoyed about. They actually have a good reason to be annoyed about his lying as it is causing them a few headaches. For example, at a school football match, parents come up to Pete (his father) and he realises that Ben has been telling them lies about him, such as that he is a SAS bodyguard and how he is so bravely fighting. Ben is the middle child in the Brockman family, and he is a hyperactive and crafty kid. In Series 1, he had developed an extraordinary talent for lying, which his parents were clearly annoyed about. They actually have a good reason to be annoyed about his lying as it is causing them a few headaches. For example, at a school football match, parents come up to Pete (his father) and he realises that Ben has been telling them lies about him, such as that he is a SAS bodyguard and how he is so bravely fighting. Ben is a very eccentric, stubborn, unusual, nosy, immature, and funny child - he often refuses to do what his parents tell him to. Pete and Sue often give up with Ben. He is very clever and is exceedingly skilled at chess. He is well versed in science though his curiosity and impulsivity towards the subject has been known to cause 'health and safety' issues within the classroom. He has a passion for history and often quotes random historical facts. He also seems to know a lot about religion despite not being religious himself. Ben has many friends at school which he plays games with usually involving rounding people up. His form tutor notes that he is very popular. He sometimes can be argumentative sometimes with his siblings, mostly Karen, but overall the three get along.

characters.name: Brick Bollinger
characters.description: Brick BollingerLocationPhoenix, Arizona, USAAgeUnknownGenderMaleSeries InformationFirst SeenThe Restaurant Brick(his actual name) was the mad, pompous, and dangerous husband ofAngela Morrison(and two before). He lived in Phoenix with Angela with their five children and he cheated on his wives. He début inThe RestaurantSeries 3. Later he was trying to get Misty and Angela back to America. Misty said that he used to keep her up in her room for hours while Angela described him as a monster (given his manipulative. overbearing behavior). Brick also made a speech in the episode calledThe Restaurantin Series 3.Jake BrockmanandPeter Brockmanhad a bet about if Brick had a ponytail and if he said he phrase "low self-esteem issues."

characters.name: Brick's time with Angela
characters.description: Brick Bollingeris Angela's wonderful husband in series nice man. He first appears as Angela's husband, with several children. In his first appearance, he brings his youngest daughters, June and Jean and sons Plymouth and Mustang with him. He constantly berates and harasses Pete, constantly calling him Hugh Grant. It is obvious that the only reason Angela took Brick with her back to Britain was for leverage to encourage jealousy in her sister. He is incredibly confident, and his pathetic philosophies leave all of the Brockmans speechless...except for Karen, who responds defensively when he calls her sweetheart. Pete points out that Brick's methods are highly unlikely to work on Karen, and Brick mutters that she has a lot of defenses. It is revealed that he is a sex-addiction therapist, and Angela tries to use this to mock Pete. But, however, Brick is shown to be paedophilic and expresses, covertly, to Pete that, as men, it is within their right to nail as many women as they can. However, after calling him Hugh Grant one time too many, Pete violently threatens to shove Brick's Platinum Visa card right up his backside, which triggers Taylor-Jean's emotional distress that she wants her real mom, not Angela. It also sets off a massive brawl between Brick and Pete, which is very narrowly defused by Sue and Angela. Later in the series, Brick is revealed to be abusive and sadistic, as he bullies his female children, locking them in dark rooms for many hours and intimidating them. He then threatens to prosecute Sue and Pete if they don't hand Angela and Misty over to him. In the end, for some reason, Angela remarries Brick.

characters.description: Charlene at work Charlenewas a not very bright receptionist because of this she only worked on Christmas day. She has a austrailan accent. She was portrayed by Daisy Haggard

characters.name: Deion
characters.description: Deionis Ben's friend who appears in Series One, Episode TwoThe Special Bowland comes round for tea with Deion with Ben and Karen Karen and Ben. He lies about his dad and how he went to prison. Pete and Sue notice that he is lying and blame him for Ben's lying habits, but Deon later confesses that Ben told him to lie.

characters.name: Dentist
characters.description: Dentistappeared in theThe Broken Santa, and was portrayed by Nicholas Kahn. He told Karen he wasn't a Christian and he didn't celebrate Christmas - so he filled in for other dentists. He only worked on Christmas, and came third in his year whichKarenseemed less than impressed with.

characters.name: Draxi
characters.description: Draxi is a Croatian woman that babysat Ben and Karen in The Night Out. Draxi She allowed Karen and Ben to stay up late. Draxi was seen to be very lenient and spent a lot of time on her phone.She also claims that Ben's hair is strong, like a buffalo.

characters.name: Frank Morrison
characters.description: Frank MorrisonFrank in series oneLocationUnknown Address (burnt down)AgeUnknownGenderMaleSeries InformationFirst SeenThe City FarmLast SeenThe Broken Santa  Frank Morrison, commonly known asGrandad, is a fictional charater in the television series,Outnumbered, and was portrayed byDavid Ryallbefore his passing in 2014. His fictional character is confirmed deceased in the 2016 Christmas special episode. Frank was the father ofAngelaandSue, father-in-law toPete, and grandfather ofJake,Ben, andKaren. He suffered from early stages of dementia. He spent most of his time in quiet contemplation; when he did engage in conversation, he would normally repeat a story he already told. In the second series, Frank comes to stay with the Brockmans, while he recovers from a nasty accident involving a tin of baked beans - he inadvertently set his kitchen on fire. Frank must come to terms with the fact he is losing his memory. He is easily agitated over TV programs, mostlyThe Weakest Link, as in the Series 2 episodeThe Night Out, shouts at the screen "Bank! Bank it!" over and over. In the end of series two, he has to move to a residential care home. During a Christmas special, he makes friends with a fellow inmate at the stately home, calledMack. They both keep wandering off from the home and visiting the pub.

characters.name: Grayson
characters.description: Grayson is a boy who is an alleged chess prodigy who plays against Ben inThe Internetand "has never lost a local tournament" and eventually succumbs to Ben's antics during the chess tournament, to the dismay of his mother (the referee), it is possible that he, along with Ben in the aftermath ofThe Internet, was banned from the school chess league.

characters.name: HMS Belfast Official
characters.description: HMS Belfast is the boat/ship that the Brockmans Were on in The Family Outing. Karen was doing her WW2 Project. The Prize was a trip on HMS Belfast.

characters.name: Howard
characters.description: Howard's reaction to Pete lashing out on him Howard was an anti-speed bump campaigner who turned up at the family's door on several occasions, trying to get petitions from people to get rid of the speed bumps the council is planning on building down the road. He was eventually shouted at byPetewhen he turns up at the house right after Pete learnsKarenhas been knocked down by a car and is in hospital. He was played by Alex MacQueen in the episodesThe Tennis MatchandThe Hospital.

characters.name: Ibrahim
characters.description: Ibrahimis one ofBen's friends.Ibrahimand Ben get up to loads of mischief together. InThe Internet, it's mentioned that Ben sold him the jawbone of a Chinese soldier (during the Korean War) given to him by his grandfather Frank. InThe Coldcaller,he gave his ventriloquist dummy to Ben for the school talent show. It used to belong in his family attic, but they didn't want it anymore; after his grandmother said it was the work of the Devil.

characters.name: Jake Brockman
characters.description: Jake BrockmanLocation19 Keely Road, London (Series One - 2016 Special)AgeUnknownGenderMaleSeries InformationFirst SeenThe School RunLast SeenChristmas 2024 Jacob Peter Brockmanmore commonly asJakeis a fictional character in the television series,Outnumbered, and is portrayed byTyger Drew-Honey. He was born between January and August 1996. Jake is the oldest son of Pete and Sue. In many ways, Jake is the most sensible member of the family, as well as being the oldest child out of the three. He tends to be rather more serious than a teenage boy should be, leading him to be very cynical and sarcastic. He seems to know something about everything, possibly because he spends most of his time using his computer, which does occasionally get him into trouble. Jake is the oldest son of Pete and Sue. In many ways, Jake is the most sensible member of the family, as well as being the oldest child out of the three. He tends to be rather more serious than a teenage boy should be, leading him to be very cynical and sarcastic. He seems to know something about everything, possibly because he spends most of his time using his computer, which does occasionally get him into trouble. Even though he does things himself that are wrong, he manages to turn things around on his parents, shown especially in theChristmas Specialwith music downloads, also inThe Restaurantwhere Sue believes he is seeing Kelly as a 'Slab of meat' where as Kelly sees him as a very mature and very sexy guy, believing him to be 16. Jake often lies about his age when he has a crush on a girl; in Series 3 he tellsKellyhe's 16, and in Series 4 he tells his girlfriendVictoriahe was 17 (although she too lied about her age). Despite he's maturer than Ben and Karen he can give Pete and Sue attitude but more to his dad.

characters.name: Jane
characters.description:
Jane is a cheesy brain as stated by her child, Alexa.
She is a very annoying parent who is always shoving her daughter Alexa onto the Brockmans and never picks her up when she promises to. Sue always plans to tell her that she is annoying but never does because she always starts feeling sorry for her. Jane is also troubled because her ex-husband sends her abusive messages gloating about witholding access to her daughter on Christmas day. She says this has caused her to lose her confidence and she wasn't always like this. She is shown to have limited patience as shown when Karen kept talking to her while she was trying to defecate about her goat. She’s great

characters.name: Jane Brockman
characters.description: Age Unknown Sisters

characters.name: Jo
characters.description: JoLocationUnknownAge12 (When last appearance)Genderfemale Jois Jake's friend who appears in some episodes. She and Jake often do homework together, go on The Sims game in Jake's room, and play outside. She is a very mature, clever, sporty and a pretty girl who cooks meals for her family and plays clarinet for the English National Youth Orchestra. Jo never appered in the Third Series possibly meaning that her and Jake have fallen out after he wouldn't cover for her. She was his first crush

characters.name: Julie
characters.description: Julie is Sue's cousin who appears inThe Weddingon her wedding day. The Brockmans Sue,Pete,Jake,BenandKarenarrive at the Wedding where Julie is the bride, and Karen is a bridesmaid. Julie is getting married and is very nervous. Unsuprisingly, Sue takes offence at these remarks and kicks Angela.

characters.name: Karen Brockman
characters.description: Karen BrockmanLocation19 Keely Road, London (Series One - 2016 Special)Age23GenderFemaleSeries InformationFirst SeenThe School RunLast SeenChristmas 2024 Karen Rose Brockman, more commonly known as Karen, is Pete and Sue's youngest child and only daughter. She was born either during or not long after September 2001, so she is now 23. She has a talent for interrogation, often managing to ask an adult the most bizarre , off-the-wall question which completely throws them off balance. Karen is a sweet, bright, and talkative girl who is very inquisitive and tends to spend much of her time asking questions about anything and everything. Karen often annoys her family members with her quick wit and her "literal" attitude. She spends quite a lot of time in her room, acting out reality TV shows with her stuffed toys such as Britain's Got Talent, Hell's Kitchen, The X Factor & The Apprentice. She also occasionally draws slightly disturbing pictures. Karen is extremely intelligent but also extraordinarily idealistic. This is shown when she argues with her mother over how to hit a boy in a 'ladylike' way. She clashes with Ben because of their dual personalities and Karen almost viciously reacts when she thinks she is being pitied or belittled. Karen is also cynical: Her idealism leads her to cling to her beliefs and she becomes angry or confused when she is challenged, but also effortlessly brings down anybody's sense of superiority. This is shown in her relationship with her aunt, Angela. Some of Karen's innocent questions are actually rather probing and, if answered correctly, would be rather embarrassing for the adults involved. In her younger days, Karen had an interest in everything, including nits. At one point she wanted to keep one as a pet. Karen still doesn't yet subscribe to the 'being polite' social skills that adults must endure - and thus Karen doesn't hide the fact she doesn't really like her Auntie Angela. She displays this by saying in a game of who can tell the biggest lie, " I like Auntie Angela". She is shown to be very cunning and intelligent in The Old-Fashioned Sunday when her parents try and get out listening to her play the recorder and she tricks them in to listening. Her intelligence is also shown in The Family Outing when she asks Pete if her and Gran were going to argue and cleverly points out that her and Pete were arguing as they were not agreeing on the same thing. However, her outgoing and optimistic attitude is finally snapped when, after she comes third in a swimming race, which she usually excels at, she explodes that she lost and believes it is because she came from a "family of losers." Karen is a sweet, bright, and talkative girl who is very inquisitive and tends to spend much of her time asking questions about anything and everything. Karen often annoys her family members with her quick wit and her "literal" attitude. She spends quite a lot of time in her room, acting out reality TV shows with her stuffed toys such as B

characters.description: Laurenis a classmate ofKaren.Once Karen went over to Lauren's house. After Karen went over Lauren's father said that people like the woman cold calling should be shot. She was portrayed byIsabel Rosen

characters.name: Lucie
characters.description: Lucieis one ofBarbara's children. She is her only named child. She is the youngest, and is probably the same age as Lucie inThe Dead Mouse Karen. After all Barbara's children say "Hi Sue" she says it last.

characters.name: Mack
characters.description: MackAgeOver 60GenderMale Mackis a friend ofFrank's from the retirement home. He is originally from Scotland and speaks with a broad Scottish accent. He could have possibly burgled the Brockman's House, as it was revealed he used to be a burglar. He once was found in a pub bySueandJakein a pub when he and Frank had escaped from the home and the two were looking for him. Both men ended up coming to the Brockman's house for lunch, despite Mack being uninvited.

characters.name: Maddy
characters.description: Maddyis and Aunt to Sue and Pete's children she is mentioned inJake's Secret (Part 1)when Karens says that Maddy's Family The Brockmans' once Maddy slept in Karen's bed and wet it because she had too much red wine. Karen called her a boarderline Acholic and she broke rules 1, 4, 6 and 8.

characters.name: Marcus
characters.description: Marcus (Outnumbered) Marcus is a friend of Jake's and the older brother of Tanya. He makes an appearance in the fourth episode of series 4 (The Parent's Evening). He joins Jake during a scene in one of the school's music rooms for a band practice. Karen joins them because Pete and Sue are attending Ben's parent's evening and there would be nobody at home to care for her.

characters.name: Mary
characters.description: Mary is a character who appeared in The Wedding in season 2 where she was Julie's bridesmaid. Her parents are divorced and she has three pets, which Karen is very envious of. It is not mentioned that she's related to Julie
